Empires & Puzzles Dubhán Review: 5-Star Dark/Purple Hero

Dubhán brings a unique 'reverse-decay' chain attack that hits harder with every jump. But with stats that are starting to show their age, can he still compete with the modern monsters? Read our deep dive to find out.
Dubhán, The Thundering Storm: Ramping Up The Pain
Dubhán is a 5-star Dark (Purple) Paladin from the

family, originally released during the Monster Island event. While he might look like he's dragging a ship's anchor to a sword fight, his primary gimmick is a "chain lightning" style attack that punishes teams for being too healthy. He operates on a unique "reverse-decay" mechanic where his hits get
as they bounce, rather than weaker—essentially the anti-Anne. If you like seeing big numbers pop up on the screen in ascending order, this is your guy.

Dubhán sports a rugged, tribal aesthetic consistent with the Plains Hunter theme. He's wielding a massive, chained weapon that looks like a cross between a hook and a halberd, which he presumably uses to fish for whales or very large goblins. The purple war paint and intense expression suggest he takes his job of "hitting things until they stop moving" very seriously. It's a solid design that screams "brute force" rather than "subtle magic."
When we look at Dubhán's stats in the harsh light of 2026, he is definitely showing his age. His base power of
sits well below the modern standard of ~1260+.
His 1095 base attack is low compared to modern hitters who push 1300+ easily. Even with Limit Breaks, he lags behind the cutting edge.
His bulk is his saving grace. A base HP of
is still quite respectable, and as a Paladin, he can take a hit. However, his defense stat is merely average by today's inflated standards.
He needs full Limit Breaks and Master Emblems just to keep pace with the survivability of modern base-level heroes.
This skill is a fascinating gamble. Unlike most chain hitters (like Anne or Finley) who lose power with each bounce, Dubhán gains it.
. This is a double-edged sword. It makes him an excellent opener or cleaner against a fresh team, but a terrible finisher against a team clinging to life.
The damage increases significantly on each hit. Community testing suggests jumps of roughly
. If he manages to hit 5 enemies, that last hit is a nuclear event.
The bleed damage isn't huge (roughly 339-498 total), but the
is a nice cherry on top. It's not enough to be your primary healer, but it keeps him and his neighbors topped up.
Let's break down the potential output.
Average (10 tiles). With a Lv 29 Magic/Styx troop or Lv 23/29 Mana troop + Class/Costume bonus, you can shave this down to 9 tiles.
This is the "Dream Scenario." In reality, you will rarely hit all 5 enemies because they won't all be above 50% HP. Realistically, expect 2-3 hits.
If Bleed deals ~500 damage per enemy over 3 turns.
Heal = 30% of 1500 = 450 HP split between him and neighbors. (It's a trickle, not a flood).

family offers some interesting utility, particularly for survivability.
The Attack bonus (up to +20% for 5 heroes) is nice but hard to capitalize on unless you're running a mono-Hunter team, which is rare. The defense/stat boost during the

Adds a small DoT (150 damage over 3 turns) to a random enemy. It's negligible damage in the grand scheme of things but can help strip "ignore damage below X" buffs or finish off 1 HP enemies.

When Dubhán released, he was a "fun" alternative to the dominant meta heroes. He was never quite the "must-have" that heroes like Jove or Aramis were, but he found a niche as a punisher for heavy-healer defenses.

The game has moved towards Faster speeds and heavier hitting "Hit All" heroes. Average speed for a chain hitter is risky in a world where 6-tile rush teams exist. His damage ramp is still potent, but getting him to fire
the enemy decimates you is the challenge.
He is no longer a top-tier defense hero. In offense, he shines in
or against passive, healer-heavy teams where enemies stay above 50% HP for a long time. Against modern aggressive defenses, he is often too slow and his stats too soft.
As a Paladin, Dubhán has natural bulk. However, his job is to KILL.
Maximizes the damage of his ramping chain. If that 4th or 5th hit lands, you want it to delete the enemy, not just tickle them. Also boosts his Bleed damage.
Slightly less survivability, but his Paladin class helps mitigate this.
Makes him a meat shield. Good if you are using him solely for the Bleed/Heal utility or as a tank (not recommended anymore).
Wastes his potential. A 300% hit on the first target with defensive stats is pathetic.
. Dubhán is a damage dealer first and foremost. If he isn't killing things, he isn't doing his job.
Excellent against teams that rely on overhealing or constant healing.
The damage ramp-up is one of the highest in the game for chain attacks. Hitting the 4th or 5th enemy is devastating.
High defense potential and natural tankiness.
Provides a small layer of sustain for your team.
If enemies are below 50% HP, the chain breaks. This makes him awkward to use mid-game or as a finisher.
In a Fast/Very Fast meta, Average speed is a liability.
You cannot rely on his damage reduction passive for strategy.
Low base attack means his multipliers have to work harder to impress.
This is his playground. At Very Fast speed, he can fire early while enemies are still at full health, maximizing his chain potential.
The stat boost makes him a premier choice here.
Great for clearing waves of mobs that spawn with full health.
Pair him with a defense down hero who DOESN'T deal massive damage (or fires after him). Actually, you want him to fire
or after a non-damaging defense down (like
In Rush wars, pair him with other Average/Slow heavy hitters.
. Ludwig charges him, he wipes the team.
Overheal because it keeps enemies above 50% HP, ensuring his chain continues.
A Taunt hero stops his chain dead on the first hit.
If he misses a link in the chain, the chain stops.
Don't pair him with heroes who deal "some" damage to everyone (like a weak AoE) just before him. You might drop enemies below 50% HP and break his chain condition.

If you pulled Dubhán early, he is a solid carry. His Paladin stats will keep him alive, and his chain attack can wipe entire teams in lower-level raids. Focus on keeping him alive and firing him at the start of the battle.

He is a luxury toy. Unless you are desperate for a Rush War purple hero or want to optimize your Monster Island score, he is probably not worth the Alpha Aethers. There are simply better, faster, and harder-hitting purples available (like
). Keep him at 3/70 or 4/80 for niche use unless you love his mechanic.
The community reaction to Dubhán was a mix of confusion and pleasant surprise regarding his damage mechanic.
One player noted, "It’s like the opposite of Anne, but better... I had to read it three times before realizing damage increases each hit lol."
Users calculated his hits to be roughly "300, 400, 500, 600 and probably 700," confirming that he is indeed a beast if the stars align.
Many players overlooked the heal-on-bleed mechanic, which, while small, "keeps him and his neighbors topped up."

Dubhán is a hero who lives for the highlight reel. When his chain connects on 5 heroes and that last number pops up huge, you feel like a genius. The rest of the time, you're cursing him for hitting one guy and stopping because an enemy was at 49% HP. He is a fun, chaotic, and unique hero that has sadly been power-crept by stats, but his mechanic remains one of the most satisfying in the game.
A situational star. Amazing in Rush and PvE, but too inconsistent for the main stage of competitive play.
